ACTOR1: Military Forces of the United States (2021-2025)
SOURCE: Al Jazeera; Al Masdar; Ansar Allah; AP; Arab News; France24; Times of Israel; UKMTO; Yemen Data Project; Yemen News Agency (SABA) - Houthi
NOTES: On 19 November 2023, Houthi forces hijacked the Bahamas-flagged Galaxy Leader vehicles carrier (IMO: 9237307) in the international waters of the South Red Sea around 60 nm west of Al Hudaydah claiming Israeli affiliation of the ship. Houthi forces landed a helicopter on the ship and brought the ship to Al Hudaydah taking the 25 crew members hostage. The ship was officially owned by an Isle of Man-registered company but its beneficial owner was an Israeli-registered company linked to Israeli shipping magnate Abraham 'Rami' Ungar. Houthi forces announced the release of the crew members on 22 January 2025 in support of the ceasefire agreement in Gaza.
SOURCE_BASIS: AIS/Dryad Global - Galaxy Leader at Hodeidah anchorage

SOURCE: Aden al Ghad; USCENTCOM
NOTES: Interception: On 23 November 2023, US Navy destroyer USS Thomas Hudner shot down several suspected Houthi one-way attack drones during a patrol in the South Red Sea. The incident took place as Houthi forces had been claiming several rounds of drone and missile attacks targeting Israel in the context of the latest round of violence in Israel-Palestine.
SOURCE_BASIS: Zone-based: South Red Sea shipping lane

NOTES: On 31 December 2023, helicopters from US Navy aircraft carrier USS Dwight D. Eisenhower and destroyer USS Gravely responded to Houthi crew served and small arms fire from 4 Houthi small boats in the South Red Sea around 60 nm northwest of Al Hudaydah. The clash resulted in the sinking of 3 of the 4 Houthi boats with Houthi forces admitting the killing of 10 Houthi fighters. The incident took place as the US helicopters were responding to a distress call of the Maersk Hangzhou container ship (IMO: 9784300) after the Houthi boats attempted to board the ship and exchanged fire with the armed security team onboard. The Maersk Hangzhou was also struck by a Houthi missile the day prior with Houthi forces claiming that it was headed to Israel (coded as a separate event). The Israeli destination of the ship was not verified.

NOTES: On 29 September 2025, around 1:20 pm Sanaa time, Houthi forces fired 1 cruise missile targeting the Minervagracht general cargo ship (IMO: 9571521) in the Gulf of Aden, around 128 nm southeast of Aden, claiming it violated the Houthi ban to call at Israeli ports. The missile hit the ship, igniting a fire on board, and causing it to be abandoned and left adrift. Two sailors were injured in the attack. A French warship evacuated all 19 crew members and the security team of the ship, who included sailors of Russian, Ukrainian, Sri Lankan, and Filipino nationalities. The ship had no known Israeli affiliation and independent sources did not corroborate the Houthi claim that it had called at Israeli ports recently. The Filipino crew member died of his injuries on 7 October. This was the second attack targeting the ship, with the first attempt taking place a week earlier on 23 September, and failing to hit the ship (coded separately).
